name: SUNHYDROGEN, INC.
business: SunHydrogen is developing an efficient and cost-effective way to produce truly renewable hydrogen using sunlight and any source of water. The company is currently working to scale lab-scale prototypes to commercial-scale and has not yet generated revenues. Its technology is primarily developed at its independent laboratory in Coralville, Iowa, with support from university and industrial partners.
namedSuppliers: Project NanoPEC consortium (Fraunhofer CSP, WAVELABS, ECH Elektrochemie Halle, Zahner-Elektrik, Helmholtz-Zentrum Berlin)
University of Texas at Austin Center for Electromechanics (UT-CEM)

exposures.sentence: SunHydrogen is developing an efficient and cost-effective way to produce truly renewable hydrogen using sunlight and any source of water.

SunHydrogen is a shareholder in Norway-based TECO Fuel Cell Technology (formerly TECO 2030), a company that has demonstrated both innovation and resilience in its mission to accelerate the global transition to zero-emission energy.

catalysts.event: Filed provisional patent application for CdTe Photovoltaic Module Systems and Methods for Autonomous Water Electrolysis
catalysts.sentence: On August 7, 2024, we filed a provisional patent application titled CdTe Photovoltaic Module Systems and Methods for Autonomous Water Electrolysis.

catalysts.event: Filed provisional patent application for Photoelectrochemical Reactor for Hydrogen Production
catalysts.sentence: On November 25, 2024, we filed a provisional patent application titled Photoelectrochemical Reactor for Hydrogen Production.

catalysts.event: Filed non-provisional and PCT patent application for CdTe Photovoltaic Module Systems and Methods for Autonomous Water Electrolysis
catalysts.sentence: On August 6, 2025, we filed a non-provisional and PCT patent application titled CdTe Photovoltaic Module Systems and Methods for Autonomous Water Electrolysis with the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO).

catalysts.event: Engaged TPG Engineers for FEED of a 25 m^2 pilot plant and entered collaboration with UT-CEM for 30 m^2 multi-panel pilot system
catalysts.sentence: During 2025 we engaged TPG Engineers for front-end engineering design (FEED) of a 25 m^2 pilot plant, and we entered a collaboration with the University of Texas at Austin Center for Electromechanics (UT-CEM) to host and operate a 30 m^2 multi-panel pilot system.

tamClaims.claim: Renewable hydrogen market could reach $1.4 trillion per year by 2050
tamClaims.sentence: Deloitte projects that the renewable hydrogen market could reach $1.4 trillion per year by 2050, driven by rising demand for decarbonization across sectors.

tamClaims.claim: Clean hydrogen investment of $7.8 billion in 2025
tamClaims.sentence: The International Energy Agency (IEA) forecasts $7.8 billion in clean hydrogen investment in 2025 a 70% increase over 2024 with projects at the Final Investment Decision (FID) stage set to expand production capacity to 7.5 million tonnes annually by 2035.

tamClaims.claim: Production capacity will expand to 7.5 million tonnes annually by 2035
tamClaims.sentence: The International Energy Agency (IEA) forecasts $7.8 billion in clean hydrogen investment in 2025 a 70% increase over 2024 with projects at the Final Investment Decision (FID) stage set to expand production capacity to 7.5 million tonnes annually by 2035.

tamClaims.claim: Current pipeline could deliver around 44 million tonnes of clean hydrogen by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: Deloitte estimates that the current pipeline could deliver around 44 million tonnes of clean hydrogen by 2030 about a quarter of expected demand highlighting both the progress made and the scale of opportunity ahead.
